event: A Huguenot couple embracing on St. Bartholomew's Day
symbols: ivy-covered wall — faithfulness and attachment that endures
white armband the woman tries to tie — the Catholic badge that would save him, which he refuses
embrace — love torn between earthly attachment and spiritual conviction
garden setting — a fragile Eden about to be shattered by violence
description: A Pre-Raphaelite painting showing a young couple in an intimate embrace against a brick wall covered with green ivy. The man wears a purple doublet and black cap, while the woman in a dark dress with yellow accents presses against him, appearing to tie or remove something from his arm. She gazes up at him with tender pleading, while he looks down at her with gentle resolve. The setting is a garden with a brick wall, climbing ivy, and small flowers on the ground. The painting has the characteristic luminous detail and rich coloring of the Pre-Raphaelite movement.

_meta.title: A Huguenot, on St. Bartholomew's Day, Refusing to Shield Himself from Danger by Wearing the Roman Catholic Badge
